summary: Comprehensive testing of Inventory Management and Parts Requests features. All backend API tests passed (16/16). All frontend UI tests successful for Parts Requests page (status filter, expand, status advancement), Item Master tabs (Categories, Products with bulk upload buttons, Bundles, Inventory with stock levels, adjustment, and history).
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/admin/inventory - lists inventory with stock levels
Backend: GET /api/admin/inventory?search=cat6 - search functionality
Backend: POST /api/admin/inventory/adjust - stock adjustment (purchase type)
Backend: POST /api/admin/inventory/adjust - stock adjustment (return type)
Backend: GET /api/admin/inventory/{product_id}/history - transaction history
Backend: GET /api/admin/item-master/bulk-upload/sample - CSV download
Backend: POST /api/admin/item-master/bulk-upload - creates products
Backend: POST /api/admin/item-master/bulk-upload - duplicate SKU detection
Backend: GET /api/admin/parts-requests - list all requests
Backend: GET /api/admin/parts-requests?status=quoted - filter by status
Backend: Parts requests contain items with pricing details (GST, totals)
Backend: PUT /api/admin/parts-requests/{id}/status - status advancement
Backend: Parts requests show engineer info and quotation links
Backend: GET /api/admin/item-master/products - list products
Backend: GET /api/admin/item-master/categories - list categories
Frontend: Parts Requests page loads with status filter pills (All, Pending, Quoted, Approved, Procured, Delivered)
Frontend: Parts Requests expand on click showing items table, engineer info, quotation link
Frontend: Parts Requests status filter works (Quoted filter shows 5 requests)
Frontend: Status advancement button ('Mark as Delivered') visible in expanded view
Frontend: Item Master page has 4 tabs: Categories, Products, Bundles, Inventory
Frontend: Products tab shows 'Sample CSV', 'Bulk Upload', 'Add Product' buttons
Frontend: Inventory tab shows stock levels (In Stock, Purchased, Used, Price)
Frontend: Inventory shows Low Stock indicator for items with 0 stock
Frontend: Inventory search filters products (cat6 search returns 1 item)
Frontend: Inventory row click shows history panel with transaction log
Frontend: Stock Adjustment modal has Type, Quantity, Unit Cost, Notes fields
Frontend: Stock adjustment successfully updates inventory (108 → 111)

features: Status filter pills (All, Pending, Quoted, Approved, Procured, Delivered)
Expandable rows showing items table, pricing, engineer info
View Quotation button when quotation is linked
Mark as Approved/Procured/Delivered status advancement buttons
Sample CSV download button on Products tab
Bulk Upload button opens file picker
Duplicate detection (rejects duplicate SKU or product name)
Table with columns: Product, Category, SKU, In Stock, Purchased, Used, Price, Adjust
Low Stock indicator (red text) for items at/below reorder level
Row click opens history panel on right
Stock adjustment modal with Type, Quantity, Unit Cost, Notes
Transaction history shows type (initial, purchase, return, used), quantity, date, user
Summary showing In Stock, Purchased, Used counts
Transaction log with type badges (initial, purchase, return, used)
Job details (ticket number, company name) for 'used' transactions

context_for_next_testing_agent: All Inventory and Parts Requests features tested and working. Test data includes 9 products in inventory with various stock levels, 6 parts requests mostly in 'quoted' status. Stock adjustment tested multiple times - Cat6 Ethernet Cable now at 111 units. The inventory auto-deduct feature is in engineer_visits.py checkout when resolution='fixed'. No mocked APIs - all endpoints are real.
